verdict_reason: The disagreements_among_translators block flags a genuinely doctrinally important divergence: avyayām is the same word used for the imperishable Self in 2.17 / 2.21, and Krishna deploys it here in deliberate mirror — the actor cannot harm the imperishable Self in others, but can manufacture as their own imperishable signal-pattern. Modern translators ('always speak of your infamy' — Prabhupada) flatten avyayām; Shankara and Ramanuja preserve it as 'unending.' The explanation names the doctrinal stake clearly — the difference between moralistic 'always speak' and structural 'imperishable infamy attaches to the name.' Substantive PASS.
